rulings.Case Title: WEST BUILDERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Museum Plaza, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Integrated Design Engineers, Llc Into Arbitration Pursuant To Written Stipulation And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Integrated Design Engineers, Llc And Its Counsel Of Record Pursuant To Ccp 128.5
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 9, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC.'S Motion To Compel Integrated Design Engineers, LLC Into Arbitration Pursuant To Written Stipulation And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Integrated Design Engineers, LLC And Its Counsel Of Record Pursuant To CCP 128.5. Defendant Museum Plaza, LLC's Motion to Compel Arbitration is granted. Defendant Integrated Design Engineers signed agreement to arbitrate the instant controversy. (Decl. of Lucas J. Olona, Ex. A.) Five out of the six named parties to the arbitration agreement executed the agreement. The agreement, therefore, "reflects a clear intent to submit the case to arbitration . . . ." (Porreco v. Red Top RV Center (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 113, 120.) Defendant Integrated Design Engineers does not meet its burden to demonstrate that the arbitration agreement excludes the present dispute from arbitration. It does not sufficiently assert a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. (Code Civ. Pro. §1281.2.) Defendant Museum Plaza LLC's Motion for Monetary Sanctions is denied. This Court has not previously ordered arbitration. In 2010, it merely stayed litigation pending arbitration. D Integrated Design Engineers, therefore, did not necessarily act in bad faith by opposing arbitration. There is insufficient evidence presented of Plaintiff's alleged unsuccessful attempts to meet and confer with Defendant Integrated Design Engineers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Nonmonetary And Monetary Sanctions For Discovery Misconduct Against Prime Old County L.P., Prime Old County Acquisition And Homan & Stone
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 9, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA's Motion For Nonmonetary And Monetary Sanctions For Discovery Misconduct Against Prime Old County L.P., Prime Old County Acquisition And Homan & Stone Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Defendant Prime is bound by its verified written discovery responses and my not present evidence at trial to contradict those responses. CACI 204 shall be given to the jury re spoliation of evidence. Sanctions for $2,000 which is the estimated amount of time and costs wasted by plaintiff attributable to the unprepared PMK deponent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Stephen Sturla And Daniel Marc Bornstein And Exclusion Of Expert Testimony
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 9, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Stephen Sturla And Daniel Marc Bornstein And Exclusion Of Expert Testimony Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted as to document requests to Bornstein 18, 19 (if available) and 20. As to request 6: Bornstein must provide a list of all publications and, if not otherwise reasonably available to plaintiff from other sources, provide copies on request at plaintiff's expense. As to 21: Bornstein must provide documents sufficient to show what percentage of his annual income is derived from Bay Property Group. As to 6, Sturla must provide a list of all publications and, if not otherwise reasonably available to plaintiff from other sources, provide copies on request at plaintiff's expense. Defendants failed to provide admissible evidence that production of the requested documents would be burdensome. Sanctions awarded against defendants in the amount of $3,000. rulings.Case Title: LORI RUSINKO VS. DONALD MAC DONALD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 9, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The court denies the motion for summary judgment. Defendant fails to maintain its initial burden of production per CCP 437c. Gov't Code secs. 830.4 and 830.8 do not bar this action because lack of signage/signals is merely one component of the alleged defective condition of public property. In addition, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the condition was a trap. Defendant fails to show that the design immunity defense of Gov't Code sec. 830.6 applies. The Woo declaration does not sufficiently establish discretionary approval of the design, including the lag time between when northbound and southbound traffic stop. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether a dangerous condition existed, and whether defendant had actual and/or constructive notice of the alleged defects. Defendant created the timing for the lights. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GEORGE HAUSER ET AL VS. 60 RAUSCH STREET HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 9, 2015, Line 8, PLAINTIFF GEORGE HAUSER, CHRISTINE HAUSER, RAUSCH INVESTMENT LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication. Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. "There is a triable issue of material fact if, and only if, the evidence would allow a reasonable trier of fact to find the underlying fact in favor of the party opposing the motion in accordance with the applicable standard of proof." [See Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 C4th 826, 850.] Here, the Settlement Agreement states: the HOA and Plaintiffs agree to sell eight (8) exclusive use easements for the Disputed Parking Spaces within a three year period. [UMF 3.] Plaintiffs have presented evidence that under the terms of the Settlement Agreement, Defendants must convert the disputed parking spaces non-appurtenant to Unit 404. [UMF 17.] A trier of fact could find that the Settlement Agreement allows for three years before Defendant's nonperformance constitutes a breach. Therefore, because there is a triable issue of material fact, the motion for judgment is denied. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT PIETRAFESA VS. GRETCHEN W KAH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Protective Order To Prevent Plaintiff From Entering Defendant'S Home
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 9, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT GRETCHEN KAH'sd Motion For Protective Order To Prevent Plaintiff From Entering Defendant'S Home Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Plaintiff's attorney and/or his experts and consultants may enter defendant's home to inspect the items listed in the Second Amended Demand for Inspection. The inspection may be videotaped or photographed. Plaintiff shall have ten days to review the video and/or photos. Following Plaintiff's review, he may designate any items which he believes require further personal inspection by him to be delivered--at Plaintiff's cost-- to his attorney's office for further inspection. Such items shall be delivered within ten days of Plaintiff's counsel's written notice to Defendant's counsel. Plaintiff shall then have twenty days to personally inspect and return the requested items. Alternatively, at Defendant's option, Plaintiff may enter Defendant's house for inspection on ten days notice on the condition that Plaintiff be accompanied by his attorney and that any communication may only be between the parties' attorneys. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL TINNISH VS. IMAD ABUMERI M.D ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 9, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT BAKERSFIELD NEUROSCIENCE & SPINE INSTITUTE'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. Defendant Bakersfield Nueroscience and Spine Institute's Amended Notice of Motion to Change Venue is denied. Defendant Bakersfield Nueroscience and Spine Institute incorrectly asserts that "none of the parties reside in San Francisco" as Defendant Dignity Health resides in San Francisco. Defendant Bakersfield Nueroscience and Spine Institute's reliance on Brown v. Superior Court (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 488 is misplaced. The venue provisions for actions against individual defendants and actions against corporations are not "directly in conflict." (Davis v. KGO-T.V., Inc. (1998) 17 Cal.4th 436, 446, disapproved on other grounds in Williams v. Chino Valley Independent Fire Dist. (2015) 61 Cal.4th 97.) Defendant Bakersfield Nueroscience and Spine Institute does not sufficiently the general rule that venue is proper in the county of any defendant's residence." (Delgado v. Superior Court (1977) 74 Cal.App.3d 560, 563.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: CLIFFORD E. CURRY VS. JUSTIN DUGUAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 9, 2015, Line 14, DEFENDANT JUSTIN DUGUAY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The motion is granted. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate proper service of the defendant. In addition, this appears to be a housing matter and all future filings in this case should be directed to department 501. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
